name: Bagels for Bread Machine
recipeInstructions:
Set the machine for the dough cycle. Let it knead the dough once, then let the dough rise for only 20 minutes in the machine. If your cycle runs longer, remove the dough after 20 minutes and turn off the machine. Divide the dough into the appropriate number of pieces. Roll each piece into a rope and form it into a circle, pressing the ends together. You may need to slightly wet one end to help seal the ends. Place these on a well-greased baking sheet, cover, and let rise for 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring water to a slight boil in a nonaluminum pan (a cast iron frying pan works well), about 2 inches deep. Carefully lower about 3 or 4 bagels at a time into the boiling water and cook for about 30 seconds on each side. Remove the bagels with a slotted spoon, drain on a towel, and sprinkle with poppy seeds, sesame seeds, or dried onion bits if desired. Place them on the greased baking sheet.
Bake in a preheated oven at 550°F (or 275°C) for 8 minutes.
